api: When I Work API Documentation v1.0.0 (real OpenAPI 3.1 spec at https://apidocs.wheniwork.com/external/monolith/docs-master.json; monolith service, 70 documented paths)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the live OpenAPI 3.1 document (fetched 2026-07-03). Covers 14 read streams (the 4 legacy-parity streams plus sites, blocks, annotations, availabilityevents, requesttypes, times, timezones, payrolls, openshiftapprovalrequests, swaps) and 26 write actions (create/update/delete for users, locations, positions, sites, blocks, annotations, availabilityevents, times; create+delete for shifts). Excluded: account/admin management (single-tenant account settings, not a per-record object stream), the import subsystem (async CSV-import job lifecycle, not a synchronous read/write record operation), punch/clock-in-out and shift-swap-workflow action endpoints (stateful workflow transitions, not plain CRUD), bulk/notify/publish/unassign shift actions (bulk or notification side-effecting operations with no single-record equivalent), user invite/avatar/alerts/bulkupdate (account-provisioning side effects, not data sync), v3 shift-break-audits/scheduled-breaks (newer, narrower v3 surface duplicating data already reachable off the v2 shifts stream), and login (credential-exchange, not a syncable resource). See docs.md Known limits for the real auth-scheme mismatch this bundle carries forward from legacy (token-exchange JWT vs. this bundle's Basic auth).

excluded.reason: single-user detail; identical record shape to the users list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

account-provisioning side effect (resends an email invite), not a data-record mutation
notification-dispatch side effect, not a data-record mutation
multipart image upload; this dialect has no multipart body support (JSON/form only)
bulk multi-user update over the same collection update_user covers per-record; this dialect expresses single-record writes only
authenticated caller's own user profile; same record shape already reachable in the users list stream
authenticated-caller self-update, not a collection-scoped mutation

deletes an account-wide time-off-request TYPE definition (a shared taxonomy value, not a per-record data object); Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage

bulk delete over a filter/id-list query, not a single-record path-scoped delete; delete_shift (DELETE /2/shifts/{id}) already covers per-record deletion

credential-exchange endpoint on a SEPARATE host (api.login.wheniwork.com), issues the JWT this bundle's real auth scheme would need; not a syncable object stream. See docs.md Known limits for the AUTH_COMPLEX gap this leaves open.
